subject: English
skill: Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence

lesson.story_mission: Join the Evidence Explorers to answer questions about short passages and prove each answer with text.
lesson.objective: I can answer literal and inferential questions using text evidence.
lesson.concept_explanation: Good readers answer literal and inferential questions using text evidence. They slow down, notice clues in the words or text, and explain how the clue supports the answer.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may choose an answer they like without support from the text.
lesson.real_world_connection: Text evidence helps readers explain answers in book clubs, science notebooks, tests, and class discussions.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the question, return to the passage, choose an answer, and point to the words that prove it.
lesson.mini_lesson: First decide what the question asks. For literal questions, find the exact information. For inferential questions, combine clues with what you know. Always cite evidence.

audio.text: literal question, inferential question, text evidence, question word, explain
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Watch: The question asks where Nora planted the seeds. The passage says she planted them near the fence, so near the fence is the answer.
worked_examples.passage: At sunrise, Nora filled three blue buckets with soil. She planted bean seeds near the fence because that spot got the most light. By lunch, she had watered every row.
evidence_spans: that spot got the most light

audio.text: At sunrise, Nora filled three blue buckets with soil. She planted bean seeds near the fence because that spot got the most light. By lunch, she had watered every row.
audio.label: Listen

worked_examples.text: Demo: The answer is strongest when it includes evidence. “The text says that spot got the most light” proves why Nora chose the fence.
worked_examples.correct_answer: that spot got the most light
evidence_spans: that spot got the most light

audio.text: The text says that spot got the most light.
audio.label: Listen

hint_ladder: Ask: What is the question asking?
Go back to the passage and find proof.
Make sure your answer and evidence match.

misconception_bank.unsupported_answer: Readers may choose an answer they like without support from the text.
misconception_bank.inference_without_evidence: Readers may infer without connecting to a clue in the passage.
misconception_bank.misses_text_evidence: Readers may answer correctly but miss the exact evidence.
misconception_bank.question_word_confusion: Readers may confuse who, what, where, when, why, or how.

questions.passage: At sunrise, Nora filled three blue buckets with soil. She planted bean seeds near the fence because that spot got the most light. By lunch, she had watered every row.
questions.question: What is the best inference supported by the passage?
evidence_spans: that spot got the most light
acceptable_answers: that spot got the most light
questions.prompt: What is the best inference supported by the passage?
questions.correct_answer: near the fence
hints: Reread the important words.
Use the model or clue before answering.
Check that your answer matches the whole sentence or text.
questions.explanation: The answer is supported by the evidence: that spot got the most light.
questions.feedback: Yes — near the fence fits the reading clue.

story.text: Welcome to Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence. In this mission, you will answer literal and inferential questions using text evidence while helping a reader solve a real classroom task. Listen to the story goal, look for the reading strategy, and Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches you how to answer the question and prove it with words from the passage. Read the objective, look at the visual model, and think about the watch-out mistake before answering. Press Next to continue after you can say the strategy in your own words.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example carefully because it shows the whole thinking path. Step one, read the prompt or passage. Step two, use the model to find the clue. Step three, check that the final answer matches the text before you Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo lets you try the strategy with support. Listen to the example, look at the model, and think about why the answer fits. Press Next to continue when you are ready for independent practice.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is your Practice zone for Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence. Use Read Question to hear the prompt, then reread the sentence or passage yourself. Choose your answer when you are ready, and Use the hint if you need help.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This Challenge zone asks you to apply the same strategy with a little more independence. Use Read Question first, listen for any helpful audio, and check the model before answering. Answer carefully, then continue once your choice matches the text.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This Checkpoint shows what you can do after the lesson and challenge. Use Read Question to hear the prompt, then prove your answer with the reading strategy. Answer carefully, then continue to see how your work earns growth credit.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: You reached the badge screen for Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence. Think about one strategy you used well, such as rereading, checking a clue, or proving an answer. Press Next to see your profile and keep building your Grade 3 English skills.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page summarizes your growth for Ask and Answer Questions With Text Evidence. Read your accuracy, check any misconception notes, and notice the recommended next step. Continue to Skill Practice or go back to the hub when you are ready.
profile.label: Read This Page
